proposal.why: The React Native frontend has accumulated significant technical debt and become a complex mish-mash of features, dependencies, and patterns. Rather than incrementally refactor, a complete teardown and rebuild from first principles will: - Reset the codebase to a clean, intentional architecture - Remove accumulated cruft and experimental code - Allow for deliberate, planned feature implementation - Simplify dependency management and reduce maintenance burden - Give the team a clear starting point for rebuilding with lessons learned
proposal.what-changes:
Remove entirely: - src/ui/* (all component library) - src/screens/* (all screen implementations) - src/theme/* (theming system) - src/navigation/* (navigation stack) - src/domain/* (business logic layer) - src/state/* (state management) - App.tsx (app entry point) - All React Native dependencies (react-native, expo, etc.) - All UI libraries (tailwind, react-native-paper, etc.) - All state management packages - package.json scripts related to frontend build - babel.config.cjs, tailwind.config.js, tsconfig.json (frontend configs) - android/ directory (native build) - eas.json, app.json (app configs)
Retain: - src/data/* (data layer - SQLite, types) - Backend/API infrastructure - Core domain logic that persists to database - Test infrastructure
proposal.capabilities:
Lost (temporarily): - All user-facing UI and screens - Mobile app functionality - Navigation flows - Real-time updates and subscriptions
Preserved: - Data persistence and SQLite foundation - Backend API contracts - Domain logic and business rules - Test coverage for core logic
Gaps until rebuild: - No frontend at all; backend exists in isolation - No way to interact with the system without direct API calls - No build/deployment pipeline for frontend
proposal.impact:
Developers: - Clean slate to rebuild intentionally - Reduced cognitive load from legacy patterns - Opportunity to apply learnings from previous implementation
Users: - App becomes unusable until frontend rebuilt in next phases
Testing: - Frontend tests removed; backend/API tests remain - Backend contract tests become critical for rebuild validation
Deployment: - App store builds pause until new frontend ready - Backend continues to function independently
